inner-join.label: INNER JOIN
inner-join.summary: Combine rows from two tables with matching values.

sk1.title: What is INNER JOIN?
sk1.bodyMarkdown: An **INNER JOIN** in SQL is used to combine rows from two or more tables based on a related column between them. Only rows with matching values in both tables are included in the result set.

sk2.title: How INNER JOIN Works
sk2.bodyMarkdown: Suppose you have two tables: `orders` and `customers`. If you want to see all orders along with the customer names, you can use an INNER JOIN on the customer ID column that appears in both tables. Only orders with a matching customer will be shown.

sk3.title: INNER JOIN Syntax
sk3.bodyMarkdown:
The basic syntax for an INNER JOIN is:

```sql
SELECT columns
FROM table1
INNER JOIN table2
ON table1.column = table2.column;
```
This selects rows where the join condition is true.

q1.title: What does an INNER JOIN return?
q1.prompt: Which rows are included in the result of an INNER JOIN between two tables?

options.b: Rows with matching values in both tables
options.c: All rows from the first table and matching rows from the second

q2.title: When to use INNER JOIN
q2.prompt: Select all situations where you would use an INNER JOIN.

options.a: You want only records that exist in both tables
options.b: You want all records from the left table
options.c: You want to combine data where there is a match in both tables
options.d: You want to include unmatched records

q3.title: Order the INNER JOIN steps
q3.prompt: Put the steps for writing an INNER JOIN query in the correct order.

q4.title: Write an INNER JOIN query
q4.prompt: Using the `products_catalog` dataset, write a query to select the product name and category name for all products, using an INNER JOIN between `products` and `categories` on the category ID.
